name: Strait of Hormuz
summary: Day 69 Thursday. **MOU FRAMEWORK INCLUDES EXPLICIT 'BOTH SIDES LIFT HORMUZ BLOCKADES' PROVISION; IRAN SIGNALED FIRST UNCONDITIONAL REOPENING OFFER OF CYCLE — BUT OPERATIONAL REOPENING LAGS RHETORIC.** Iran (PBS/AP/Al Jazeera/IRNA): 'reopen Strait of Hormuz if US lifts blockade and war ends'; IRGC Navy added 'safe transit will be ensured with new procedures.' This is the FIRST TIME during the 67-day arc Iran has stated unconditional intent to reopen the Strait — prior Iranian framing emphasized 'controlled passage via coordination' which preserved selective control. **Operational data DOES NOT CONFIRM diplomatic-track durability:** Hormuz transit volume 5-6 vessels/day (May 3-4) vs 138 historical norm (Strauss Center / Lloyd's List); AWRP at ~0.8-1% H&M / 7-day baseline (Lloyd's List), down from 2.5% peak but still 6-8x pre-war 0.10-0.15% norm — insurers NOT pricing pause as durable yet. **CMA CGM San Antonio struck May 5 by cruise missile** off Dubai while transiting Hormuz; multiple crew injured, vessel damaged; CBS attribution to Iran (single-source US officials, IRGC has NOT claimed). France redeployed Charles de Gaulle CSG from East Med through Suez to Red Sea/Gulf of Aden (CONFIRMED — French armed forces, Naval News, ABC/NBC); first major European naval shift of cycle; Macron called for Hormuz reopening AND spoke with Pezeshkian. The 14-21 day window for Iran formal reopening (by May 26) is now Day 1-2; if signed MOU + actual reopening occur within this window the trigger resolves YES. Brent collapsed -7% May 6 to ~$103 on MOU scoop; trading $101.95 May 7 morning; chain 'Brent <$115 sustained 5+ sessions' resolved YES; new chain 'Brent <$105 sustained 5+ sessions' Day 1. Goldman Q2 forecast cut to $90; World Bank $86 average 2026 (vs $69 in 2025). VLCC TD3C steepest 1-day drop in 5+ years on MOU news ('lead balloon' — Lloyd's List). Paper-vs-physical bifurcation IS the structural intelligence: paper markets pricing peace ~50-70%, physical/insurance ~10-20%.

active_triggers.condition: Hormuz reopens to all traffic (even partially)

Hormuz remains under Iranian selective control even within ceasefire framework — ACTIVE 2026-04-08: Iran's 'controlled passage via coordination with armed forces' framing in the ceasefire text formali
Physical-futures gap closes below $10/bbl (Dated Brent converges to Brent futures)

BUT operational reality lags: 5-6 vessels/day vs 138 norm; AWRP at 0.8-1% (still 6-8x baseline); CMA CGM San Antonio struck May 5 by cruise missile (CBS-attribution to Iran, IRGC has NOT claimed). France Charles de Gaulle CSG transits Suez to Red Sea/Gulf of Aden — first European naval shift of cycle; Macron called for Hormuz reopening AND spoke with Pezeshkian. Brent collapsed -7% May 6 to ~$103 on MOU scoop; Goldman Q2 cut to $90; World Bank $86 average for 2026. VLCC TD3C steepest 1-day drop in 5+ years on MOU news ('lead balloon' — Lloyd's List).

historical_summaries.summary: 12 signals. IRGC announced Hormuz closed to US, Israeli, and Western allied ships | Dallas Federal Reserve described closure as 'largest disruption to energy supply since the 1970s energy crisis' and 'largest in the history | Mojtaba Khamenei's first statement confirmed Hormuz would remain closed to pressure Iran's enemies

64 signals. WSJ paradigm shift CORROBORATED by CNN: 'Trump administration believes it will have to end Iran war with oil chokepoint still closed | Al-Salmi VLCC attacked at Dubai port anchorage — 31 nautical miles NW of Dubai, OUTSIDE Hormuz proper | CONFIRMED: Hormuz toll law passed FULL Majlis (not just committee)
